title: Institutional Workspaces, Collaboration, and Review
principles: Public visitors never need an account to read published public workspaces.
Private workspaces, members, assignments, comments, review notes, and activity logs remain private.
Human approval is required before evidence becomes public-eligible or a workspace becomes published.
Roles express workspace permissions but do not replace an institutional identity provider or identity management system.
Retention is preview-first and preserves evidence decisions, published summaries, member-role history, and append-only audit receipts.
Exports are read-only archive packets and do not claim successful remote ingestion.

role_definitions.analyst: Capture sources, create assignments, and add comments.
role_definitions.reviewer: Review evidence and add review notes.
role_definitions.publisher: Approve publication readiness and create export packets.
role_definitions.administrator: Manage workspaces, members, retention, and all publisher permissions.

prohibited: No hidden individual tracking or social scoring
No account provisioning or identity-provider claims
No exposure of member identities or private collaboration records through public endpoints
No automatic deletion without explicit confirmation
